customers.name: VP Fleet & Network Operations
customers.description: Leads large cross-border truck and van fleets where uptime, route economics, toll compliance, fuel control, and decarbonization all sit inside one operating system. DKV Mobility’s product domain is best understood as a European B2B on-the-road payments and fleet operations platform spanning fuel cards, EV charge cards, toll settlement, telematics, fleet software, tax refund, and adjacent vehicle services. ([dkv-mobility.com][1])
careAbout: Vehicle uptime across countries
Lowest delivered cost per kilometer
Fast, compliant toll and tax handling
Fraud-resistant driver spend controls
Smooth diesel-to-EV transition without operational fragmentation
Single pane of glass for fleet, spend, and emissions
winsThem: One commercial relationship for fuel, charging, tolls, and fleet operations
Large Europe-wide acceptance footprint for fueling and charging
Unified billing, reporting, and liquidity support through consolidated invoicing
Real-time telematics tied to refueling behavior and theft prevention
CO2e reporting and CSRD-relevant emissions visibility integrated into digital tools
theirFear: Trucks stop moving because payment, charging, or toll access fails
Fuel fraud or card misuse quietly destroys margin
They miss emissions and compliance expectations from enterprise shippers

jobsToBeDone.name: Run cross-border transport with one payment and compliance stack
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Set up a fleet so drivers can fuel, charge, pay tolls, park, and use vehicle services across Europe without cash, fragmented vendors, or manual reconciliation.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Higher vehicle uptime, lower admin cost per trip, fewer failed journeys, and tighter cost visibility.

steps.description: Issue service cards and assign spending rules by vehicle, driver, and country profile.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Card and account administration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Creates controlled access to fuel, charging, toll, and vehicle services under one customer account.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Policy and limit engine
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Sets transaction limits, service eligibility, and exception rules to reduce misuse.

steps.description: Ensure acceptance across fuel stations, EV charge points, toll domains, parking, and wash networks on key corridors.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Acceptance network orchestration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Maps where fleet assets can transact across Europe through partner networks.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Toll box and vignette management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Activates country-specific toll settlement with one operational workflow.

steps.description: Guide drivers to approved low-cost fueling or charging options and monitor refueling behavior on route.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Driver app and station finder
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Helps drivers locate stations and perform digital fueling or charging flows.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Telematics-linked route and fuel optimization
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Connects vehicle location, fuel events, and planning decisions in one loop.

steps.description: Aggregate all transactions, allocate to vehicles and routes, and surface anomalies.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Unified invoicing and cost allocation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Bundles transactions into a single invoice and reporting structure by vehicle and country.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Fraud and anomaly detection
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Flags suspicious fueling patterns, stolen card use, and route mismatches in near real time.

jobsToBeDone.name: Decarbonize fleet operations without losing control
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Introduce EVs and emissions reporting into an existing mixed-energy fleet while keeping utilization high and administrative complexity low.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Measured emissions reduction, lower transition risk, and preserved service levels during energy mix change.

steps.description: Identify routes and vehicle classes where EV or hybrid economics are workable.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Route-energy analytics
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Compares route patterns, dwell times, and energy requirements to prioritize electrification.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: TCO scenario planning
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Evaluates diesel versus EV cost structures including charging, downtime, and incentives.

steps.description: Give vehicles and drivers access to public charging and digital charging workflows.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: EV charge card and digital charging
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports public charging access and smartphone-based authentication for sessions.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Charge point network integration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides interoperable access across public CPO networks.

steps.description: Track transaction-related emissions and produce reporting usable for customer tenders and CSRD-related requests.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Carbon monitor and annual emissions reporting
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Turns energy transaction data into auditable fleet emissions outputs.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Export and API data services
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Moves emissions and transaction data into shipper, ERP, and reporting workflows.

top.name: Operating margin per kilometer
top.description: Contribution margin after energy, toll, admin, and downtime costs

children.name: Vehicle utilization
children.description: Share of planned vehicle hours spent in revenue-generating movement

children.name: Unplanned stop hours
children.description: Downtime caused by payment, toll, fueling, or charging failure

children.name: Delivered energy and toll cost
children.description: Total cost of fuel, charge, toll, parking, and related services per trip

children.name: Admin reconciliation effort
children.description: Back-office time required to reconcile one trip’s mobility spend

children.name: Spend leakage rate
children.description: Fraud, misuse, and non-recoverable exceptions as a share of mobility spend

children.name: Emissions reporting coverage
children.description: Share of fleet spend with auditable CO2e attribution

top.name: Gross profit per active enterprise fleet
top.description: Margin captured from enterprise mobility transactions and software attach

children.name: Revenue per active vehicle
children.description: Combined card, toll, telematics, software, and finance revenue

children.name: Software and service attach rate
children.description: Share of active fleets buying telematics, analytics, tax refund, or carbon products

children.name: Transaction volume
children.description: Euro value of processed mobility transactions

children.name: Cross-product penetration
children.description: Average number of product domains used per enterprise account

children.name: Net revenue retention
children.description: Expansion minus churn across existing enterprise fleets

children.name: Driver monthly active usage
children.description: Share of provisioned drivers transacting monthly

productStrategy.vision: Become the operating system for European road transport spend, compliance, and energy transition.

1_year.focus: Reliability and control across core mobility spend
1_year.productTheme: Unify payment rails, acceptance visibility, and exception handling for enterprise fleets

supporting: Share of routes with verified payment coverage

businessKPI.northStar: Gross profit per active enterprise fleet

milestones: Unify enterprise provisioning for cards, toll boxes, and digital charging
Launch fleet-level exception center for failed transactions and disputes
Tie telematics alerts directly to card controls and misuse workflows
Standardize enterprise exports and APIs for ERP and TMS ingestion

3_year.productTheme: Shift from payment processor to decision engine for route, energy, and emissions economics

customerKPI.northStar: Mobility cost per kilometer under management
supporting: Average energy price variance versus benchmark

businessKPI.northStar: Net revenue retention in enterprise transport accounts
revenueStreams: Embedded workflow fees for route-energy optimization
Tax refund and financial service fees

milestones: Deploy route and energy recommendation engine
Add contract benchmarking and station recommendation logic
Operationalize auditable CO2e reporting and tender-ready outputs
Introduce enterprise policy automation by route, vehicle, and shipper requirement

5_year.productTheme: Create a programmable mobility infrastructure layer for fleets, shippers, and partners

customerKPI.northStar: Share of fleet mobility spend orchestrated through platform policies
supporting: Automated decision rate for energy and toll choices
Working capital days improved through financial services

businessKPI.northStar: Platform gross profit from ecosystem transactions

milestones: Expose programmable policy APIs for shipper-fleet payment orchestration
Create partner marketplace for maintenance, parking, charging, and compliance services
Launch financing products tied to fleet spend and tax recovery flows
Build data network effects around route, energy price, and usage optimization

customers.name: Fleet Administrator / Operations Manager
customers.description: Owns day-to-day fleet administration in companies where fleet is important but not the whole business. DKV explicitly positions fleet management as a side-job pain point and offers software, online services, app workflows, and consolidated billing to reduce manual work. ([dkv-mobility.com][2])
careAbout: Simple administration across vehicles and drivers
No missed inspections, fines, or expired documents
Fast onboarding without IT-heavy implementation
Clear cost allocation by vehicle and employee
Basic electrification readiness
Low support burden for drivers
winsThem: Free or low-friction fleet management entry point for existing customers
Single invoice and country-normalized reporting
Master data, document, inspection, and driver management in one workflow
App-based fueling and charging to reduce lost cards and support calls
Direct linkage between transactions and fleet records
theirFear: Missing compliance deadlines because data is scattered
Spending hours in spreadsheets for monthly close
Getting blamed for fleet cost overruns they cannot see early enough

jobsToBeDone.name: Administer fleet operations with minimal overhead
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Run core fleet admin, cost tracking, and service reminders without building a custom tool stack.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Lower administrative hours, fewer compliance misses, and better cost accountability.

steps.description: Register vehicles, drivers, contracts, and service providers.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Fleet master data management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Centralizes the core records used in every downstream workflow.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Document and contract management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Stores service and compliance artifacts in one governed location.

steps.description: Monitor inspections, fines, test dates, and service events.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Compliance reminders and workflow alerts
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Prevents missed dates that trigger fines or operational risk.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Vehicle lifecycle workflows
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps acquisition, usage, and service milestones structured.

steps.description: Map transactions to vehicles and drivers for monthly close and policy enforcement.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Spend allocation and reporting
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Links mobility transactions to fleet entities for easier close.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Cockpit dashboards
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides non-technical visibility into trends, outliers, and action items.

jobsToBeDone.name: Support employees on the road without growing support headcount
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Enable drivers and sales reps to pay for mobility services digitally while keeping admin and support tickets low.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Higher user satisfaction, fewer lost receipts or cards, and lower support cost.

steps.description: Issue cards or digital access for fueling and charging based on user role.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Card plus digital wallet flows
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Allows both physical and app-led use depending on employee context.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Role-based user provisioning
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Differentiates benefits and controls across drivers, managers, and travelers.

steps.description: Let users find stations, view transactions, and complete charging or fueling through the app.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Mobile self-service app
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Reduces support dependency by putting discovery and transaction status in the user’s hand.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Digital fueling and charging
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Cuts dependency on physical cards and manual reconciliation.

steps.description: Give administrators visibility into failed transactions, blocked cards, and driver requests.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Support console and issue history
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Allows fast triage of exceptions without jumping across vendors.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Card stop and replacement workflows
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Shortens issue resolution time and limits loss exposure.

customers.name: Truck Driver / Field Driver
customers.description: The daily user of the platform’s payment and mobility access layer. DKV provides app-led station discovery, APP&GO digital fueling or charging, roadside support, and broad acceptance coverage, making the driver a critical adoption and retention node even if they are not the budget owner. ([dkv-mobility.com][1])
careAbout: Fast acceptance at the station or charger
No out-of-pocket payments or reimbursement hassle
Confidence the card or app will work abroad
Easy navigation to approved locations
Minimal waiting and clear transaction confirmation
Help when something goes wrong on the road
winsThem: Reliable station and charger access on key routes
Digital fueling and charging in app
Transaction visibility and fewer receipt headaches
Roadside and hotline support
Clear, low-friction authentication
theirFear: Being stranded because a card, app, or toll device fails
Getting accused of misuse when the workflow is confusing
Losing time at stations and missing delivery windows

jobsToBeDone.name: Complete fueling and charging stops quickly and correctly
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Find an accepted station, authenticate, transact, and continue the trip without support intervention.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Less idle time, fewer failed transactions, and better driver confidence.

steps.description: Locate approved fuel or charge points near route and compare fit for vehicle needs.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Station finder and route-aware search
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Helps drivers discover eligible stations without detouring blindly.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Acceptance and amenity metadata
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Shows whether the stop supports the right vehicle, service, and access method.

steps.description: Use card, RFID, QR, or app flow to start fueling or charging.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Multi-mode authentication
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Lets drivers use the best available method at each station.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: APP&GO transaction flow
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Removes dependence on a physical card where digital acceptance exists.

steps.description: See that the transaction was successful and move on without paper-heavy follow-up.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Digital transaction history
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides immediate confidence and lowers disputes.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Real-time exception messaging
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Tells the driver what went wrong and what fallback is available.

jobsToBeDone.name: Stay compliant and supported while on the road
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Cross borders, pay tolls, access parking or wash where needed, and get help fast when incidents occur.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Lower stress, fewer route interruptions, and better on-time performance.

steps.step: Pass toll and parking checkpoints correctly
steps.description: Use the right device or payment path for local infrastructure requirements.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Toll device interoperability
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Reduces country-by-country confusion and manual payment work.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Parking and ancillary service access
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Extends the payment credential beyond fuel into daily driver needs.

steps.description: Reach support for breakdowns, blocked cards, or transaction problems.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Hotline and roadside support integration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Gives drivers a single recovery path when something fails.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Driver identity and account recovery
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Restores access quickly after loss, theft, or misconfiguration.

productStrategy.vision: Deliver the most trusted driver interaction layer for European on-road commerce.

1_year.productTheme: Remove friction from high-frequency on-road transactions
milestones: Unify card and app identity into one driver profile
Improve station quality and acceptance metadata
Add clearer transaction recovery and offline fallback messaging
Expand digital fueling and charging coverage on core corridors

3_year.productTheme: Use real-time context to recommend the best next stop and service path

customerKPI.northStar: Minutes saved per driver per week

milestones: Launch context-aware stop recommendations
Integrate secure truck parking and amenity booking deeper into trip flow
Personalize authentication and recovery paths by station type
Expose driver quality feedback loop into network scoring

5_year.productTheme: Turn the mobile layer into an intelligent agent that books, pays, and proves compliance

supporting: Automated toll and ancillary settlement coverage

milestones: Enable agentic booking and payment for parking, charging, and wash services
Create verified digital proof of transaction and compliance for employers
Use network quality and driver behavior data to steer partner economics
Make driver experience a primary moat in account retention

customers.name: Mobility Network / Software Partner Manager
customers.description: Represents fuel stations, charge point operators, toll partners, telematics integrators, and enterprise software teams that connect to DKV’s acceptance and data layer. DKV exposes transaction, toll, and telemetry interfaces and depends on a broad partner network for service coverage. ([DKV Mobility API Portal][3])
careAbout: Incremental transaction volume from fleet customers
Fast and predictable integration
Clear settlement and operational support
Access to enterprise demand without building their own sales force
Good network visibility and discoverability
Low dispute and support burden
winsThem: High-quality B2B demand aggregation across European fleets
APIs for transaction, toll, and telemetry data exchange
Strong distribution via cards, app, and customer portal
Operational support for implementation and issue handling
Ability to monetize acceptance without owning the full fleet relationship
theirFear: Integration cost outweighs incremental transaction volume
Poor data quality creates disputes and settlement friction
They become a commodity while DKV captures all customer value

jobsToBeDone.name: Integrate into DKV demand and settlement flows
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Connect services, expose availability or transaction data, and get included in the fleet operating workflow.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Incremental volume, lower go-to-market cost, and predictable settlement.

steps.description: Implement APIs or operational data feeds for transactions, pricing, or telemetry.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Settlement and dispute workflows
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps partner operations scalable as volume grows.

steps.description: Make services visible and usable inside DKV customer and driver workflows.

capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Increases discoverability to the right fleet cohorts.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Quality scoring and recommendation engine

jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Bring mobility transaction, toll, and emissions data into ERP, TMS, telematics, and reporting products.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Higher workflow stickiness for software partners and stronger ecosystem dependence on DKV.

steps.description: Pull transaction, toll, and telemetry records in consistent formats.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Normalized data APIs

steps.description: Use the data for route optimization, finance close, carbon reporting, or compliance workflows.

productStrategy.vision: Build the dominant mobility infrastructure ecosystem for Europe’s fleet economy.

1_year.productTheme: Make it materially easier to integrate, certify, and monetize through DKV

revenueStreams: Partner placement and demand routing value

milestones: Ship partner onboarding console and certification toolkit
Normalize pricing and transaction data across partner classes
Create partner quality score feeding station and service discovery
Standardize dispute workflows across fuel, charging, and toll domains

milestones: Launch partner marketplace across maintenance, parking, charging, and compliance
Expose policy and routing APIs to enterprise software partners
Enable co-selling and lead-sharing motions for strategic partners
Monetize analytics and benchmarking products built on aggregated network data
